resources.title: Digital Storage Oscilloscope
resources.subtitle: Real-time waveform capture and analysis
resources.description: Keysight DSO series digital storage oscilloscopes used for capturing, displaying, and analysing electronic waveforms in digital circuit experiments.
resources.overview: The Keysight Digital Storage Oscilloscopes in the Digital Circuits Lab provide students with real-time 2-channel waveform capture and analysis capabilities. These instruments are central to almost every lab experiment, enabling students to visualise clock signals, logic transitions, and timing diagrams directly from their circuit breadboards and PCBs.

resources.title: DC Regulated Power Supply
resources.subtitle: Stable voltage and current source for circuit experiments
resources.description: Dual-channel regulated DC power supplies providing stable, adjustable voltage and current for powering digital circuit breadboard experiments.
resources.overview: The DC Regulated Power Supplies in the Digital Circuits Lab support dual-channel output, allowing students to power TTL and CMOS circuits simultaneously with independently adjustable voltage rails. They are used in every experiment involving discrete logic gates, flip-flops, multiplexers, and adder circuits.
applications: Powering TTL and CMOS logic circuits
Constant current source for sensor experiments

resources.title: Keysight Handheld Multimeter
resources.subtitle: Portable DMM for voltage, current, and resistance
resources.description: Keysight handheld digital multimeters used for quick in-circuit voltage, current, resistance, and continuity measurements.
resources.overview: Each bench station in the Digital Circuits Lab has a Keysight handheld multimeter available. These are used alongside oscilloscopes to take static measurements of node voltages, supply rails, resistor values, and diode characteristics. Their auto-ranging capability makes them accessible for first-year students.

resources.title: Experiment Breadboard Stations
resources.subtitle: Solderless prototyping for digital circuit assembly
resources.description: Full-sized solderless breadboards at each student station for rapid assembly and testing of digital logic circuits.
resources.overview: Each student workstation in the Digital Circuits Lab is equipped with a full-size solderless breadboard. These are the primary prototyping platform for all Digital Circuits and Basic Electronics course experiments. Students use them to build combinational and sequential logic circuits, connect ICs, resistors, capacitors, and LEDs, and observe circuit behaviour in real time.
applications: Logic gate and combinational circuit assembly

resources.title: Passive Component Kit
resources.subtitle: Resistors, capacitors, and discrete components
resources.description: A wide range of resistors, capacitors, and other passive discrete components available for student experiments in the lab.
resources.overview: The Digital Circuits Lab maintains a well-stocked inventory of passive components for student use during experiments. This includes standard resistor values from 10 Ohm to 1 MOhm, ceramic and electrolytic capacitors, and LEDs. These are issued at the component counter and must be returned after experiments.
applications: Indicator LEDs for logic state display

resources.title: TTL / CMOS Logic IC Library
resources.subtitle: 7400 series and CMOS logic gates and flip-flops
resources.description: A curated library of 7400-series TTL and 4000-series CMOS logic ICs covering gates, flip-flops, counters, multiplexers, and decoders.
resources.overview: The Digital Circuits Lab maintains a comprehensive IC library covering all components required for Digital Circuits and Basic Electronics course experiments. Students can issue ICs from the counter including AND, OR, NAND, NOR, XOR gates (74LS series), D and JK flip-flops, 4-bit binary counters, 7-segment decoder drivers, and 4-to-1 multiplexers.

specs.val: AND, OR, NAND, NOR, XOR, NOT

applications: Flip-flop timing and state machine experiments
